description: Evaluates PKI and cryptographic key management maturity—centralized platforms, certificate and key lifecycle, machine identity, custodianship, and KPIs/KRIs.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A PKI and cryptographic key management service exists and is reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise PKI and key management service exists

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: PKI and key management KPIs and KRIs are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Certificate expiry and renewal compliance metrics
Short-lived and automated certificate adoption metrics
Unknown or shadow certificate discovery metrics
Key rotation and expiry compliance metrics
HSM and cloud KMS coverage metrics
Orphaned and unknown key inventory metrics

capabilities.name: Encryption and key management policy
capabilities.description: Enterprise-wide cryptographic procedures and key provisioning and deprovisioning policies are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Centralized PKI, KMS, and HSM platform
capabilities.description: Centralized certificate, KMS, and HSM platforms inventory and manage cryptographic assets enterprise-wide with quarterly configuration review.
evaluation_focus: Integration for certificate generation, distribution, and revocation
Key management solution or cloud KMS for cryptographic keys
HSM or hardware-backed key protection for high-value keys
Regular key management solution configuration review
Integration with data management and certificate management

capabilities.name: Certificate lifecycle operations
capabilities.description: Certificate creation, distribution, and revocation are formalized, scoped appropriately, automated with protocols like ACME and SCEP, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Automated certificate creation, distribution, and revocation

capabilities.name: Key lifecycle management
capabilities.description: Key provisioning, rotation, state management, and revocation operate with documented processes and compromise response reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Regular key provisioning and deprovisioning process review
Enterprise-wide key activation and deactivation processes
Automatic key activation from change management triggers
Key suspension based on compromise triggers
Enterprise-wide key archival and recovery policies
Enterprise-wide key revocation process and procedures
Automated key revocation based on triggers
Remove or deprovision keys when compromised
Regular key revocation and compromise process review

capabilities.name: Machine identity and short-lived credentials
capabilities.description: Machine identity uses short-lived certificates and cloud KMS governance with quarterly review.
evaluation_focus: Short-lived certificate policy for workloads and APIs
Certificate transparency or discovery scanning for shadow certs
Governed cloud KMS usage across cloud providers
BYOK and HYOK policies for cloud encryption
Workload and service key lifecycle in cloud KMS
Separation of human-administered and machine-managed keys

capabilities.name: Custodianship and CEK roles
capabilities.description: Certificate and encryption key custodians and CEK roles are defined with separation of duties.
evaluation_focus: Clear CEK management roles and responsibilities

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
